title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ الْاوَّلُ فِي الْغَزَلِ وَشَكْوَىٰ الْغَرَامِ
title.english: Chapter One: Nostalgic Rhapsody and Love Complaint
title.urdu: پہلا باب: نعتِ رسول ﷺ اور اظہارِ محبت

chapters.description: This chapter expresses the poet's intense love and yearning for the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, using classical Arabic poetic metaphors of place, tears, and longing.

verses.arabic:
أَمِنْ تَذَكُّرِ جِيرَانٍ بِذِي سَلَمِ
مَزَجْتَ دَمْعًا جَرَى مِنْ مُقْلَةٍ بِدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Amin tadhakkuri jīrānin bi dhī salami
Mazajta damʿan jarā min muqlatin bidami
verses.english:
Is it from remembering neighbours at Dhū Salam
That you shed tears admixed with blood from your eyes?
verses.singable:
Is it from thinking of neighbors past in Dhu Selemi
That you have mixed blood in tears that flow from eyes steadily?

verses.arabic:
أَمْ هَبَّتِ الرِّيحُ مِنْ تِلْقَاءِ كَاظِمَةٍ
وَأَوْمَضَ البَرْقُ فِي الظَّلْمَاءِ مِنْ إِضَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Am habbatir rīhu min tilqāʾi kādhimatin
Wa awmaḍal barqu fīẓ ẓulmāʾi min iḍami
verses.english:
Or because wind has blown from the direction of Kāẓima
And lightning has flashed in the dark of Iḍam?
verses.singable:
Or blowing of wind from the direction of Kazimah,
And lightning flashing from Idum in the dark that you see?

verses.arabic:
فَمَا لِعَيْنَيْكَ إِنْ قُلْتَ اكْفُفَا هَمَتَا
وَمَا لِقَلْبِكَ إِنْ قُلْتَ اسْتَفِقْ يَهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Famā li ʿaynayka in qultakfufā hamatā
Wa mā li qalbika in qultastafiq yahimi
verses.english:
So what ails your eyes that when you tell them ‘stop’, they continue to weep still more?
And what ails your heart that when you bid it awake, it wanders further in distraction?
verses.singable:
So what is wrong with your eyes: when you say "Stop!" they just weep?
And with your heart: when you say, "Be sane!" it acts crazily?

verses.arabic:
أَيَحْسَبُ الصَّبُّ أَنَّ الحُبَّ مُنْكَتِمٌ
مَا بَيْنَ مُنْسَجِمٍ مِنْهُ وَمُضْطَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ayaḥsabuṣ ṣabbu annal ḥubba munkatimun
Mā bayna munsajimin minhu wa muḍṭarimi
verses.english:
Does the lovelorn man think that love can be hidden
Behind a torrent from his [eyes], or a heart’s raging fire?
verses.singable:
Does one so lovestruck imagine love is concealable,
Between a downpour from it and being so fiery?

verses.arabic:
لَوْلاَ الهَوَى لَمْ تَرِقْ دَمْعًا عَلَى طَلَلٍ
وَلاَ أَرِقْتَ لِذِكْرِ البَانِ وَالْعَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lawlal hawā lam turiq damʿan ʿalā ṭalalin
Wa lā ariqta li dhikril bāni wal ʿalami
verses.english:
But for passion, you would not shed tears at an abandoned camp,
Or lie awake at night recalling the fragrant willow or the mountain;
verses.singable:
If not for passion, you would have never burst into tears
At the remains, nor lost sleep over the mount or the tree.

verses.arabic:
فَكَيْفَ تُنْكِرُ حُبًّا بَعْدَمَا شَهِدَتْ
بِهِ عَلَيْكَ عُدُولُ الدَّمْعِ وَالسَّقَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fakayfa tunkiru ḥubban baʿda mā shahidat
Bihi ʿalayka ʿudūlud damʿi was saqami
verses.english:
So how can you deny this love when tears and sickness,
truthful witnesses, have testified to it against you?
verses.singable:
So how do you still deny a love to which testified
Against you true witnesses of tears and infirmity;

verses.arabic:
وَأَثْبَتَ الوَجْدُ خَطَّيْ عَبْرَةٍ وَضَنىً
مِثْلَ البَهَارِ عَلَى خَدَّيْكَ وَالعَنَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa athbatal wajdu khaṭṭay ʿabratin wa ḍanā
Mithlal bahāri ʿalā khaddayka wal ʿanami
verses.english:
Lovesickness has etched two lines, of tears and gauntness,
Upon your cheeks, like yellow spice and red ʿanam
verses.singable:
When grief has fixed on your cheeks canals of tears and malaise
Like branches of dogwood, red, and daffodils, yellowy?

verses.arabic:
نَعَمْ سَرَى طَيْفُ مَنْ أَهْوَى فَأَرَّقَنِي
وَالحُبُّ يَعْتَرِضُ اللَّذَّاتِ بِالأَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Naʿam sarā ṭayfu man ahwā fa arraqanī
Wal ḥubbu yaʿtariḍul ladhdhati bil alami
verses.english:
Yes, my beloved’s apparition came to me and denied me sleep,
For love always opposes pleasures with pain!
verses.singable:
Yes, sights of my love have come at night and kept me awake;
How love repels all delights and comforts with agony!

verses.arabic:
يَا لاَئِمِي فِي الهَوَى العُذْرِيِّ مَعْذِرَةً
مِنِّي إِلَيْكَ وَلَوْ أَنْصَفْتَ لَمْ تَلُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā lāʾimī fīl hawal ʿudhriyyi maʿdhiratan
Minnī ilayka wa law anṣafta lam talumi
verses.english:
O you who blame me for this chaste love, pardon me!
But had you judged fairly, you would not have blamed me.
verses.singable:
You critic of love so passionate, an apology
From me to you-but if you were true, you'd not censure me.

verses.arabic:
عَدَتْكَ حَالِي لاَ سِرِّي بِمُسْتَتِرٍ
عَنِ الْوُشَاةِ وَلاَ دَائِي بِمُنْحَسِمِ
verses.transliteration:
ʿAdatka ḥāliya lā sirrī bi mustatirin
ʿAnil wushāti wa lā dāʾī bimunḥasimi
verses.english:
May you be spared my condition! My secret cannot be concealed
From detractors, nor is there any cure for my ailment!
verses.singable:
May you be spared what I bear-my secret isn't concealed
From haters, nor is there any end to my malady.

verses.arabic:
مَحَّضْتَنِي النُصْحَ لَكِنْ لَسْتُ أَسْمَعُهُ
إِنَّ المُحِبَّ عَنْ العُذَّالِ فِي صَمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Maḥḥaḍtanī nuṣḥa lākin lastu asmaʿuhu
Innal muḥibba ʿanil ʿudhdhāli fī ṣamami
verses.english:
You have given me sincere advice, but I cannot hear it;
A lover’s ears are deaf to the reproaches of critics.
verses.singable:
You've offered me true advice, but simply, I hear it not;
The lover is deaf to all of those who speak critically.

verses.arabic:
إِنِّي اتَّهَمْتُ نَصِيحَ الشَّيْبِ فِي عَذَليِ
وَالشَّيْبُ أَبْعَدُ فِي نُصْحِ عَنِ التُّهَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Innit tahamtu naṣīḥash shaybi fī ʿadhalī
Wash shaybu abʿadu fī nuṣḥin ʿanit tuhami
verses.english:
I hold suspect even the advice of my own grey hairs,
Even though their advice is far from deception.
verses.singable:
I've even doubted gray hair's advice when it censures me;
Although gray hair with advice is less in dubiety.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ الثَّانِ فِي الحْذِيرِ مِنْ هَوَى النَّفْسِ
title.english: Chapter Two: Warnings about the Caprices of the Ego
title.urdu: دوسرا باب: نفسانی خواہشات پر قابو پانا اور نصیحت

chapters.description: This chapter serves as a warning against the temptations of the ego (nafs) and outlines the importance of self-restraint and spiritual discipline.

verses.arabic:
فَإِنَّ أَمَّارَتيِ بِالسُّوءِ مَا اتَّعَظَتْ
مِنْ جَهْلِهَا بِنَذِيرِ الشَّيْبِ وَالهَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa inna ammāratī bis sūʾi mat taʿaẓat
Min jahlihā bi nadhīrish shaybi wal harami
verses.english:
Because of its ignorance, my ill-commanding ego has paid no heed
To the warner of grey hair and approaching old age;
verses.singable:
My evil ego, in its stupidity, paid no heed
To all the warning of graying hair and seniority.

verses.arabic:
وَلاَ أَعَدَّتْ مِنَ الفِعْلِ الجَمِيلِ قِرَى
ضَيْفٍ أَلَمَّ بِرَأْسِي غَيْرَ مُحْتَشِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lā aʿaddat minal fiʿlil jamīli qirā
Ḍayfin alamma bi raʾsī ghayra muḥtashimi
verses.english:
Nor has it prepared good deeds in welcome
For an unannounced guest who has settled on my head.
verses.singable:
And hasn't set out a spread of pleasing actions to host
A guest who showed at my head, bereft of timidity.

verses.arabic:
لَوْ كُنْتُ أَعْلَمُ أَنِّي مَا أُوَقِّرُهُ
كَتَمْتُ سِرًّا بَدَاليِ مَنْهُ بِالكَتَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Law kuntu aʿlamu annī mā uwaqqiruhu
Katamtu sirran badālī minhu bil katami
verses.english:
Had I realised, I would not have honoured it;
I would have used [black] katam-dye to hide what it showed
verses.singable:
And had I known that I'd fail to honor him, I'd have hid-
Den using hair colorant his first appearance to me.

verses.arabic:
مَنْ لِي بِرَدِّ جِمَاحٍ مِنْ غَوَايَتِهَا
كَمَا يُرَدُّ جِمَاحُ الخَيْلِ بِاللُّجُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Man lī biraddi jimāḥin min ghiwāyatihā
Kamā yuraddu jimāḥul khayli bil lujumi
verses.english:
Who will help me restrain my bolting ego from its wilfulness
As a rebellious horse may be restrained with reins?
verses.singable:
Who's there for me to control the fight of its wandering-
Just as a bit bridles horses in their obstinacy?

verses.arabic:
فَلاَ تَرُمْ بِالمَعَاصِي كَسْرَ شَهْوَتِهَا
إِنَّ الطَّعَامَ يُقَوِّي شَهْوَةَ النَّهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Falā tarum bil maʿāṣī kasra shahwatihā
Innaṭ ṭaʿāma yuqawwī shahwatan nahimi
verses.english:
Do not try to thwart unlawful desires by satisfying them,
For food only increases a glutton’s desires.
verses.singable:
So do not count on offenses to get rid of its lust;
Food only intensifies the craving of gluttony.

verses.arabic:
وَالنَّفْسُ كَالطِّفِلِ إِنْ تُهْمِلْهُ شَبَّ عَلَى
حُبِّ الرَّضَاعِ وَإِنْ تَفْطِمْهُ يَنْفَطِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wan nafsu kaṭṭifli in tuhmilhu shabba ʿalā
Ḥubbir raḍāʿi wa in tafṭimhu yanfaṭimi
verses.english:
The ego is like a child: neglect it and it will grow up loving
To suckle; but if you wean it, it will be weaned.
verses.singable:
The ego is like a baby: leave it and it grows up
In love with suckling; but wean it, and it's weaned finally.

verses.arabic:
فَاصْرِفْ هَوَاهَا وَحَاذِرْ أَنْ تُوَلِّيَهُ
إِنَّ الهَوَى مَا تَوَلَّى يُصْمِ أَوْ يَصِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Faṣrif hawāhā wa ḥādhir an tuwalliyahu
Innal hawā mā tawallā yuṣmi aw yaṣimi
verses.english:
Divert its vain desires and beware of giving it power,
For vain desires pollute or destroy whatever they control.
verses.singable:
So fight its whim, and beware of giving power to it;
When whim's empowered, it kills or damages markedly.

verses.arabic:
وَرَاعِهَا وَهِيَ فِي الأَعْمَالِ سَائِمَةٌ
وَإِنْ هِيَ اسْتَحْلَتِ المَرْعَى فَلاَ تُسِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa rāʿihā wa hiya fīl aʿmāli sāʾimatun
Wa in hiyastaḥlatil marʿā falā tusimi
verses.english:
Guard it as it grazes in the field of actions;
And should it find the grazing sweet, do not let it roam.
verses.singable:
And shepherd it carefully as it is grazing in deeds;
And if the pasture you find it fancies, don't let it be.

verses.arabic:
كَمْ حَسَّنَتْ لَذَّةً لِلْمَرْءِ قَاتِلَةً
مِنْ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَدْرِ أَنَّ السُّمَّ فِي الدَّسَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kam ḥassanat ladhdhatan lil marʾi qātilatan
Min ḥaythu lam yadri annas summa fid dasami
verses.english:
How often it has found delight in something fatal,
For [one] cannot tell that there is poison in the fat!
verses.singable:
How many times it's convinced a man of killer delights-
While blind to the poison in the gravy so savory!

verses.arabic:
وَاخْشَ الدَّسَائِسَ مِنْ جُوعٍ وَمِنْ شِبَعٍ
فَرُبَّ مَخْمَصَةٍ شَرُّ مِنَ التُّخَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wakhshad dasāʾisa min jūʿin wa min shibaʿin
Farubba makhmaṣatin sharrun minat tukhami
verses.english:
Beware of the snares of hunger and of satiety,
being hungry at times can be worse than gorging oneself!
verses.singable:
Beware the ambush of being hungry and being full;
Starvation at times is even worse than satiety.

verses.arabic:
وَاسْتَفْرِغِ الدَّمْعَ مِنْ عَيْنٍ قَدِ امْتَلَأَتْ
مِنَ المَحَارِمِ وَالْزَمْ حِمْيَةَ النَّدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wastafrighid damʿa min ʿaynin qadimtalaʾat
Minal maḥārimi walzam ḥimyatan nadami
verses.english:
Empty out the tears from an eye that has filled itself up
with forbidden sights. Maintain a strict diet of remorse.
verses.singable:
And void of tears eyes that have been stuffed with forbidden things.
Keep in the shelter of pure repentance unceasingly.

verses.arabic:
وَخَالِفِ النَّفْسَ وَالشَّيْطَانَ وَاعْصِهِمَا
وَإِنْ هُمَا مَحَضَاكَ النُّصْحَ فَاتَّهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa khālifin nafsa wash shayṭāna waʿṣihimā
Wa in humā maḥaḍākan nuṣḥa fat tahimi
verses.english:
Oppose the self and Satan and disobey them;
If they offer you advice, be suspicious of them.
verses.singable:
And contradict both the Self and Devil, and disobey!
And if they give true advice to you, still look skeptically.

verses.arabic:
وَلاَ تُطِعْ مِنْهُمَا خَصْمًا وَلاَ حَكَمًا
فَأَنْتَ تَعْرِفُ كَيْدَ الخَصْمِ وَالحَكَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lā tuṭiʿ minhumā khaṣman walā ḥakaman
Fa anta taʿrifu kaydal khaṣmi wal ḥakami
verses.english:
Obey neither of them, whether they oppose or give judgment,
For you know the wiles of opponent and judge.
verses.singable:
And do not settle with either as your lawyer or foe;
You're well aware of a lawyer's and a foe's treachery.

verses.arabic:
أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ مِنْ قَوْلٍ بِلاَ عَمَلٍ
لَقَدْ نَسَبْتُ بِهِ نَسْلاً لِذِي عُقُمِ
verses.transliteration:
AstaghfiruLlāha min qawlin bilā ʿamalin
Laqad nasbtu bihi naslan lidhī ʿuqumi
verses.english:
I seek Allah’s forgiveness for words bereft of deeds,
As by them I attributed progeny to a sterile man.
verses.singable:
I ask for pardon from God for empty word with no deed;
I have ascribed by it to a sterile man progeny.

verses.arabic:
أَمَرْتُكَ الخَيْرَ لَكِنْ مَا ائْتَمَرْتُ بِهِ
وَمَا اسْتَقَمْتُ فَمَا قَوْليِ لَكَ اسْتَقِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Amartukal khayra lākin māʾtamartu bihi
Wa māstaqamtu famā qawlī lakastaqimi
verses.english:
I enjoined goodness upon you while failing to heed the same.
I was not upright, so what use is my enjoining uprightness?
verses.singable:
I've ordered you to do good, but have ignored it, myself;
I'm not upstanding, so who am I to tell you to be?

verses.arabic:
وَلاَ تَزَوَّدْتُ قَبْلَ المَوْتِ نَافِلَةً
وَلَمْ أُصَلِّ سِوَى فَرْضٍ وَلَمْ أَصُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lā tazawwadtu qablal mawti nāfīlatan
Wa lam uṣalli siwā farḍin wa lam aṣumi
verses.english:
I have not gathered provisions of voluntary prayers before death,
or prayed and fasted beyond what is obligatory.
verses.singable:
I haven't packed extra deeds in preparation for death;
I haven't prayed, haven't fasted, but the compulsory.

title.english: Chapter Three: Praise of the Prophet ﷺ
title.urdu: تیسرا باب: حضورِ اقدس ﷺ کی تعریف و توصیف

chapters.description: This chapter contains profound, highly-revered expressions of praise for the Prophet Muhammad's ﷺ character, devotion, and lofty spiritual status.

verses.arabic:
ظَلَمْتُ سُنَّةَ مَنْ أَحْيَا الظَّلاَمَ إِلَى
أَنِ اشْتَكَتْ قَدَمَاهُ الضُّرَّ مِنْ وَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ẓalamtu sunnata man aḥyādh ẓalāma ilā
Anishtakat qadamāhuḍ ḍurra min warami
verses.english:
I have wronged the Sunna of him who enlivened the dark nights
By praying till his feet complained of painful swelling.
verses.singable:
I've violated the way of him who livened the dark,
Until his feet had complained of swelling up painfully;

verses.arabic:
وَشَدَّ مِنْ سَغَبٍ أَحْشَاءَهُ وَطَوَى
تَحْتَ الحِجَارَةِ كَشْحًا مُتْرَفَ الأَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa shadda min saghabin aḥshāʾahu wa ṭawā
Taḥtal ḥijārati kash-ḥan mutrafal adami
verses.english:
He placed a stone over his belly and soft skin,
Tightening a belt over it to diminish the hunger pangs.
verses.singable:
And out of hunger, would bind his abdomen and would wrap
Beneath a hard stone a waist whose skin is so velvety.

verses.arabic:
وَرَاوَدَتْهُ الجِبَالُ الشُّمُّ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ
عَنْ نَفْسِهِ فَأَرَاهَا أَيَّمَا شَمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa rāwadat-hul jibālush shummu min dhahabin
ʿAn nafsihi fa arāhā ayyamā shamami
verses.english:
Lofty mountains tried, to tempt him with turning to gold,
But he showed them the height of his own loftiness!
verses.singable:
The lofty mountains made into gold for him tried to be
A way to bait him, so he true loftiness let them see.

verses.arabic:
وَأَكَّدَتْ زُهْدَهُ فِيهَا ضَرُورَتُهُ
إِنَّ الضَّرُورَةَ لاَ تَعْدُو عَلَى العِصَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa akkadat zuhdahu fīhā ḍarūratuhu
Innaḍ ḍarūrata lā taʿdū ʿalal ʿiṣami
verses.english:
His dire neediness only strengthened his detachment;
neediness like his never leads to transgression.
verses.singable:
His "poverty" only strengthened his disinterest in them;
For never are protectees assaulted by poverty.

verses.arabic:
وَكَيْفَ تَدْعُو إِلَى الدُّنْيَا ضَرُورَةُ مَنْ
لَوْلاَهُ لَمْ تُخْرَجِ الدُّنْيَا مِنَ العَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa kayfa tadʿū ilad dunyā ḍarūratu man
Lawlāhu lam tukhrajid dunyā minal ʿadami
verses.english:
How could need tempt him with worldliness, when but for him
The world would not have emerged from non-existence?
verses.singable:
How could it tempt to this world-the "poverty" of him who
If not for him, the whole world would not have been made to be.

verses.arabic:
مُحَمَّدٌ سَيِّدُ الكَوْنَيْنِ وَالثَّقَلَيْـ
ـنِ وَالفَرِيقَيْنِ مِنْ عُرْبٍ وَمِنْ عَجَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Muḥammadun sayyidul kawnayni wath thaqalay-
-ni wal farīqayni min ʿurbin wa min ʿajami
verses.english:
Muhammad is the master of both abodes, of both kinds,
And both classes [of people]: Arabs and non-Arabs.
verses.singable:
Muhammad, the master of both worlds and both beings and
Both groups: the Arabs and the non-Arab community;

verses.arabic:
نَبِيُّنَا الآمِرُ النَّاهِي فَلاَ أَحَدٌ
أَبَرَّ فِي قَوْلِ لاَ مِنْهُ وَلاَ نَعَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Nabiyyunal āmirun nāhī falā aḥadun
Abarra fī qawli lā minhu wa lā naʿami
verses.english:
Our Prophet, who commands and who forbids;
None is more true than him in saying “yes” or “no”.
verses.singable:
Our prophet, who is commander and forbidder-there is
No one more true in his saying no or yes than is he.

verses.arabic:
هُوَ الحَبِيبُ الذِّي تُرْجَى شَفَاعَتُهُ
لِكُلِّ هَوْلٍ مِنَ الأَهْوَالِ مُقْتَحَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Huwal ḥabībul ladhī turjā shafāʿatuhu
Li kulli hawlin minal ahwāli muqtaḥami
verses.english:
He is the Beloved whose intercession is hoped for,
Victorious over every terror and disaster.
verses.singable:
He is the loved one whose intercession we're hopeful of,
At every horror to hit-yes, every calamity.

verses.arabic:
دَعَا إِلَى اللهِ فَالْمُسْتَمْسِكُونَ بِهِ
مُسْتَمْسِكُونَ بِحَبْلٍ غَيْرِ مُنْفَصِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Daʿā ilaLlāhi fal mustamsikūna bihi
Mustamsikūna biḥablin ghayri munfaṣimi
verses.english:
He called [people] to Allah, so those who hold fast to him
Are holding fast to a rope that never shall break.
verses.singable:
He has invited to God, so all who grip onto him
Are gripping rope that will never fray for eternity.

verses.arabic:
فَاقَ النَبِيّينَ فِي خَلْقٍ وَفِي خُلُقٍ
وَلَمْ يُدَانُوهُ فِي عِلْمٍ وَلاَ كَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fāqan nabiyyīna fī khalqin wa fī khuluqin
Wa lam yudānūhu fī ʿilmin wa lā karami
verses.english:
He excelled the other Prophets in form and qualities;
Their knowledge and nobility did not come near his.
verses.singable:
He tops the prophets in both appearance and character;
They don't approach him in knowledge or in gen'rosity.

verses.arabic:
وَكُلُّهُمْ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ مُلْتَمِسٌ
غَرْفًا مِنَ البَحْرِ أَوْ رَشْفًا مِنَ الدِّيَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa kulluhum min rasūliLlāhi multamisun
Gharfan minal baḥri aw rashfan minad diyami
verses.english:
They all seek something from Allah’s Emissary –
handfuls from the sea, or small sips of drizzle.
verses.singable:
And all of them are receiving from Allah's Messenger
A sip from down-pouring rain or handful scooped from the sea.

verses.arabic:
وَوَاقِفُونَ لَدَيْهِ عِنْدَ حَدِّهِمِ
مِنْ نُقْطَةِ العِلْمِ أَوْ مِنْ شَكْلَةِ الحِكَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa wāqifūna ladayhi ʿinda ḥaddihimi
Min nuqṭatil ʿilmi aw min shaklatil ḥikami
verses.english:
They all stand before him, observing their limits;
Dots to his knowledge, or vowel-marks to his wisdom.
verses.singable:
And all are standing before him at their own limits fixed:
A dot of knowledge or mark from words of sagacity.

verses.arabic:
فَهْوَ الذِّي تَمَّ مَعْنَاهُ وَصُورَتُهُ
ثُمَّ اصْطَفَاهُ حَبِيبًا بَارِئُ النَّسَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa huwal ladhī tamma maʿnāhu wa ṣūratuhu
Thummaṣṭafāhu ḥabīban bāriʾun nasami
verses.english:
It is he whose meaning and form attained perfection
Then the Maker of Souls chose him for His beloved.
verses.singable:
He is the one with perfected essence and outward form;
Picked as the love of the Maker of all humanity.

verses.arabic:
مُنَزَّهٌ عَنْ شَرِيكٍ فِي مَحَاسِنِهِ
فَجَوْهَرُ الحُسْنِ  فِيهِ غَيْرُ مُنْقَسِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Munazzahun ʿan sharīkin fī maḥāsinihi
Fa jawharul ḥusni fīhi ghayru munqasimi
verses.english:
Exalted above having a rival in his perfections;
In him is the undivided essence of beauty.
verses.singable:
Beyond a partner in his so beautiful qualities;
Yes, undivided in him is beauty's whole entity.

verses.arabic:
دَعْ مَا ادَّعَتْهُ النَّصَارَى فِي نَبِيِّهِمِ
وَاحْكُمْ بِمَا شِئْتَ مَدْحًا فِيهِ وَاحْتَكِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Daʿ maddaʿat-hun naṣārā fī nabiyyihimi
Waḥkum bimā shiʾta mad-ḥan fīhi waḥtakimi
verses.english:
Eschew the claim Christians have made for their Prophet.
Then say in his praise what you will, but wisely!
verses.singable:
Avoid what Christians have claimed about their own messenger;
And give to him any praise you wish, and praise sensibly.

verses.arabic:
وَانْسُبْ إِلَى ذَاتِهِ مَا شِئْتَ مِنْ شَرَفٍ
وَانْسُبْ إِلَى قَدْرِهِ مَا شِئْتَ مِنْ عِظَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wansub ilā dhātihi mā shiʾta min sharafin
Wansub ilā qadrihi mā shiʾta min ʿiẓami
verses.english:
Ascribe whatever honour you will to his essence,
And ascribe what greatness you will to his worth;
verses.singable:
Ascribe to his person what you wish of nobility;
Ascribe to his stature what you wish of immensity.

verses.arabic:
فَإِنَّ فَضْلَ رَسُولِ اللهِ لَيْسَ لَهُ
حَدٌّ  فَيُعْرِبَ عَنْهُ نَاطِقٌ بِفَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa inna faḍla rasūliLlāhi laysa lahu
Ḥaddun fa yuʿriba ʿanhu nāṭiqun bifami
verses.english:
For the worth of Allah’s Emissary has no limit
That could be expressed by a human mouth.
verses.singable:
Indeed, Allah's Messenger's perfection has no frontier
That any speaker could ever verbalize orally.

verses.arabic:
لَوْ نَاسَبَتْ قَدْرَهُ آيَاتُهُ عِظَمًا
أَحْيَا اسْمُهُ حِينَ يُدْعَى دَارِسَ الرِّمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Law nāsabat qadrahu āyātuhu ʿiẓaman
Aḥyaʾsmuhu ḥīna yudʿā dārisar rimami
verses.english:
If his miracles were commensurate with his greatness,
The mere mention of his name would give life to dry bones.
verses.singable:
And were his marvels to match his worth in greatness, his name,
When called, would give life to bones decayed to nihility.

verses.arabic:
لَمْ يَمْتَحِنَّا بِمَا تَعْيَا الْعُقُولُ بِهِ
حِرْصًا عَلَيْنَا فَلَمْ نَرْتَبْ وَلَمْ نَهِمْ
verses.transliteration:
Lam yamtaḥinnā bimā taʿyal ʿuqūlu bihi
Ḥirṣan ʿalayna falam nartab wa lam nahimi
verses.english:
He did not try us with things that confound the mind
Out of concern for us, so we neither doubted nor strayed.
verses.singable:
He tried us not, from concern, with things that boggle the mind;
And so we haven't faced any doubt or perplexity.

verses.arabic:
أَعْيَا الوَرَى فَهْمُ مَعْنَاهُ فَلَيْسَ يُرَى
فِي القُرْبِ وَالْبُعْدِ فِيهِ غَيْرُ مُنْفَحِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Aʿyal warā fahmu maʿnāhu falaysa yurā
Fīl qurbi wal buʿdi fīhi ghayru munfaḥimi
verses.english:
Man’s mind is exhausted trying to understand his meaning;
All of them, near or far, appear as if dumbstruck.
verses.singable:
Conceiving his meaning has exhausted the universe;
So near or far, one not dumbstruck by it you will not see.

verses.arabic:
كَالشَّمْسِ تَظْهَرُ لِلْعَيْنَيْنِ مِنْ بُعُدٍ
صَغِيرَةً وَتُكِلُّ الطَّرْفَ مِنْ أَمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kash shamsi taẓ-haru lil ʿaynayni min buʿudin
Ṣaghīratan wa tukilluṭ ṭarfa min amami
verses.english:
He is like the sun: small to the eye when seen from afar,
But dazzling to the sight when seen close up.
verses.singable:
Much like the sun: from afar it does appear small to eyes,
And would impair any vision when in proximity.

verses.arabic:
وَكَيْفَ يُدْرِكُ فِي الدُّنْيَا حَقِيقَتَهُ
قَوْمٌ نِيَامٌ تَسَلَّوْا عَنْهُ بِالحُلُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa kayfa yudriku fid dunyā ḥaqīqatahu
Qawmun niyāmun tasallū ʿanhu bil ḥulumi
verses.english:
How can his reality be grasped in this world
By sleeping folk, distracted from him by dreams?
verses.singable:
And how could people asleep, distracted from him by dreams,
Begin to grasp in the present world his reality!

verses.arabic:
فَمَبْلَغُ العِلْمِ فِيهِ أَنَّهُ بَشَرٌ
وَأَنَّهُ خَيْرُ خَلْقِ اللهِ كُلِّهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa mablaghul ʿilmi fīhi annahu basharun
Wa annahu khayru khalqiLlāhi kullihimi
verses.english:
The extent of our knowledge is that he is a mortal –
And that he is the best of Allah’s creation!
verses.singable:
The peak of knowledge about him is that he is a man
And is the best of Allah's creation, entirely.

verses.arabic:
وَكُلُّ آيٍ أَتَى الرُّسْلُ الكِرَامُ بِهَا
فَإِنَّمَا اتَّصَلَتْ مِنْ نُوِرِهِ بِهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa kullu āyin atar ruslul kirāmu bihā
Fa innamat taṣalat min nūrihi bihimi
verses.english:
Every miracle produced by the noble Emissaries
Came to them by virtue of his light alone
verses.singable:
And all the marvels the noble messengers have conveyed
Have come to them from the light of Ahmad exclusively.

verses.arabic:
فَإِنَّهُ شَمْسُ فَضْلٍ هُمْ كَوَاكِبُهَا
يُظْهِرْنَ أَنْوَارَهاَ لِلنَّاسِ فِي الظُّلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa innahu shamsu faḍlin hum kawākibuhā
Yuẓ-hirna anwārahā lin nāsi fidh ẓulami
verses.english:
For he is the sun of virtue, and they are its planets;
They display its lights to mankind in the darkness.
verses.singable:
He is the Sun of esteem, to which they are as if moons:
Reflecting its lights in darknesses to humanity.

verses.arabic:
أَكْرِمْ بِخَلْقِ نَبِيٍ زَانَهُ خُلُقٌ
بِالحُسْنِ مُشْتَمِلٍ بِالبِشْرِ مُتَّسِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Akrim bikhalqi nabiyyin zānahu khuluqun
Bil ḥusni mushtamilin bil bishri muttasimi
verses.english:
How noble the qualities of a Prophet beautified by such traits:
Full of beauty, and marked by smiles and good cheer!
verses.singable:
How grand the form of a Prophet brightened by character,
Enveloped by handsomeness, distinguished by jollity!

verses.arabic:
كَالزَّهْرِ فِي تَرَفٍ وَالبَدْرِ فِي شَرَفٍ
وَالبَحْرِ فِي كَرَمٍ وَالدَّهْرِ فِي هِمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kaz zahri fī tarafin wal badri fī sharafin
Wal baḥri fī karamin wad dahri fī himami
verses.english:
Like flowers in delicate beauty, like the full moon in honour;
like the sea in generosity, like time in persistence.
verses.singable:
Like flowers in fineness, the full moon in ascendancy,
The ocean in giving, and all time in tenacity.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّه وَهْوَ فَرْدٌ مِنْ جَلاَلَتِهِ
فِي عَسْكَرٍ حِينَ تَلْقَاهُ وَفِي حَشَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annahu wa huwa fardun min jalālatihi
Fī ʿaskarin ḥīna talqāhu wa fī ḥashami
verses.english:
So majestic was his presence that even when alone
he appeared surrounded by a large army and retinue.
verses.singable:
When he's alone and you meet him, he is like one amid
An army and entourage, because of his majesty.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّمَا اللُّؤْلُؤْ المَكْنُونُ فِي صَدَفٍ
مِنْ مَعْدِنَيْ مَنْطِقٍ مِنْهُ وَمُبْتَسِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annamal luʾluʾul maknūnu fī ṣadafin
Min maʿdinay manṭiqin minhu wa mubtasami
verses.english:
It is as if precious hidden pearls, sparkling from their shells,
came from the treasure-mine of his speech and smile.
verses.singable:
From treasure chests of his eloquence and his beaming grin
Do seem to be pearls preserved within their shells preciously.

verses.arabic:
لاَ طِيبَ يَعْدِلُ تُرْبًا ضَمَّ أَعْظُمَهُ
طُوبىَ لِمُنْتَشِقٍ مِنْهُ وَمُلْتَثِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lā ṭība yaʿdilu turban ḍamma aʿẓumahu
Ṭūbā li muntashiqin minhu wa multathimi
verses.english:
No perfume can match the ground that holds his bones.
Blessed are those who breathe in its fragrance or kiss it!
verses.singable:
No scent compares to the earth embracing his blessed form;
For him who sniffs it or kisses it what felicity!

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ الرّابِعُ فِي مَوْلِدِهِ عَلَيْهِ الصَّلَاةُ والسَّلَام
title.english: Chapter Four: On The Prophet's Birth
title.urdu: چوتھا باب: حضورِ اقدس ﷺ کی ولادتِ با سعادت

chapters.description: This chapter recounts the miraculous events and cosmic signs that accompanied the birth of the Prophet ﷺ, illuminating the world and defeating darkness.

verses.arabic:
أَبَانَ مَوْلِدُهُ عَنْ طِيبِ عُنْصُرِهِ
يَا طِيبَ مُبْتَدَإٍ مِنْهُ وَمُخْتَتَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Abāna mawliduhu ʿan ṭībi ʿunṣurihi
Yā ṭība mubtadaʾin minhu wa mukhtatami
verses.english:
His birth made the purity of his pedigree evident;
O how pure were his beginning and his end!
verses.singable:
His noble birth did reveal the purity of his roots.
How pure the outset of them as well as finality!

verses.arabic:
يَوْمٌ تَفَرَّسَ فِيهِ الفُرْسُ أَنَّهُمُ
قَدْ أُنْذِرُوا بِحُلُولِ البُؤْسِ وَالنِّقَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yawmun tafarrasa fīhil fursu annahumu
Qad undhirū biḥulūlil buʾsi wan niqami
verses.english:
On that day the Persians intuited that they
Had been warned of looming misery and retribution.
verses.singable:
How pure the outset of them as well as finality!
Of the occurrence of suffering and adversity.

verses.arabic:
وَبَاتَ إِيوَانُ كِسْرَى وَهُوَ مُنْصَدِعٌ
كَشَمْلِ أَصْحَابِ كِسْرَى غَيْرَ مُلْتَئِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa bāta īwānu kisrā wa huwa munṣadiʿun
Ka shamli aṣ-ḥābi kisrā ghayra multaʾimi
verses.english:
That night the throne room of Khusraw became cracked,
Khusraw’s people, too, crumbled, never to be restored.
verses.singable:
The Arch of Khosrau had passed the night while falling apart;
Just like the status of Khosrau's peers: without unity.

verses.arabic:
وَالنَّارُ خَامِدَةُ الأَنْفَاسِ مِنْ أَسَفٍ
عَلَيْهِ وَالنَّهْرُ سَاهِي العَيْنِ مِنْ سَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wan nāru khāmidatul anfāsi min asafin
ʿAlayhi wan nahru sāhil ʿayni min sadami
verses.english:
The sacred fire, grief-stricken, breathed its last,
And the river [Euphrates] dried up out of worry.
verses.singable:
The fire was short of breath out of regret over it.
The river lost all its current out of despondency.

verses.arabic:
وَسَاءَ سَاوَةَ أَنْ غَاضَتْ بُحَيْرَتُهَا
وَرُدَّ وَارِدُهَا بِالغَيْظِ حِينَ ظَمِي
verses.transliteration:
Wa sāʾa sāwata an ghāḍat buḥayratuhā
Wa rudda wāriduhā bil ghayẓi ḥīna ẓamī
verses.english:
Sāʾa was saddened by its lake drying up;
the thirsty who went to drink there came back in a rage!
verses.singable:
It saddened Sawa to find the drying up of its lake,
And thirsty seekers returning from the place angrily.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّ بِالنَّارِ مَا بِالمَاءِ مِنْ بَلَلٍ
حُزْنًا وَبِالمَاءِ مَا بِالنَّارِ مِنْ ضَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka anna bin nāri mā bil māʾi min balalin
Ḥuznan wa bil māʾi mā bin nāri min ḍarami
verses.english:
As if the fire, from sorrow, took on the water’s wetness
And water assumed the fire’s quality of blazing.
verses.singable:
As if the fire had gotten water's wetness from grief;
And water had burnt up as a fire burns seethingly.

verses.arabic:
وَالجِنُّ تَهْتِفُ وَالأَنْوَارُ سَاطِعَةٌ
وَالحَّقُ يَظْهَرُ مِنْ مَعْنىً وَمِنْ كَلِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wal jinnu tahtifu wal anwāru sāṭiʿatun
Wal ḥaqqu yaẓ-haru min maʿnan wa min kalimi
verses.english:
There were jinn calling out, and dazzling lights
As truth was manifested in both word and reality.
verses.singable:
The jinn were speaking aloud, and lights were shining so bright;
The Truth is made manifest in signal and verbally.

verses.arabic:
عَمُوا وَصَمُّوا فَإِعْلاَنُ البَشَائِرِ لَمْ
يُسْمَعْ وَبَارِقَةُ الإِنْذَارِ لَمْ تُشَمِ
verses.transliteration:
ʿAmmū wa ṣammū fa iʿlānul bashāʾiri lam
Yusmaʿ wa bāriqatul indhāri lam tushami
verses.english:
They were blind and deaf, so the glad tidings proclaimed
Went unheard, and the warning lightning-flash was not seen.
verses.singable:
But blind and deaf they were so the happy news went unheard;
And flashes of lightning as a warning they didn't see.

verses.arabic:
مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا أَخْبَرَ الأَقْوَامَ كَاهِنُهُمْ
بِأَنَّ دِينَهُمُ المُعْوَجَّ لَمْ يَقُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Min baʿdi mā akhbaral aqwāma kāhinuhum
Bi anna dīnahumul muʿwajja lam yaqumi
verses.english:
Even after the soothsayers had informed their peoples
That their crooked religion would not endure,
verses.singable:
All after their fortune teller notified all the folk
Their old religion so crooked soon would no longer be.

verses.arabic:
وَبَعْدَمَا عَايَنُوا فِي الأُفْقِ مِنْ شُهُبٍ
مُنْقَضَّةٍ وَفْقَ مَا فِي الأَرْضَ مِنْ صَنَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa baʿda mā ʿāyanū fīl ufqi min shuhubin
Munqaḍḍatin wifqa mā fīl arḍi min ṣanami
verses.english:
And even after their eyes saw meteors on the horizon
Plunging downwards, as idols were toppled on earth;
verses.singable:
And after they eyed in the horizons the shooting stars
All crashing like statues in the earth for idolatry.

verses.arabic:
حَتَّى غَدَا عَنْ طَرِيقِ الْوَحْيِ مُنْهَزِمٌ
مِنَ الشَّيَاطِينِ يَقْفُوا إِثْرَ مُنْهَزِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ḥattā ghadā ʿan ṭarīqil waḥyī munhazimun
Minash shayāṭīni yaqfū ithra munhazimi
verses.english:
Until devils, routed, from the path of revelation,
Fled in the wake of those who had been overpowered.
verses.singable:
Until from the source of revelation, up in the sky,
One after the other, each retreating demon did flee.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّهُمْ هَرَبًا أَبْطَالُ أَبْرَهَةٍ
أَوْ عَسْكَرٍ بِالحَصَى مِنْ رَاحَتَيْهِ رُمِي
verses.transliteration:
Ka annahum haraban abṭālu abrahatin
Aw ʿaskarin bil ḥaṣā min rāḥatayhi rumī
verses.english:
Fleeing like the brave warriors of Abraha
Or the army pelted by pebbles thrown by his hand,
verses.singable:
They were when fleeing just like the heroes of Abrahah,
Or like a pelted-with-pebbles-from-his-hands infantry.

verses.arabic:
نَبْذًا بِهِ بَعْدَ تَسْبِيحٍ بِبَطْنِهِمَا
نَبْذَ المُسَبِّحِ مِنْ أَحْشَاءِ مُلْتَقِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Nabdhan bihi baʿda tasbīḥin bi baṭnihimā
Nabdhal musabbiḥi min aḥshāʾi multaqimi
verses.english:
Cast by him after glorifying Allah in the palm of his hand,
As he who glorified his Lord was cast from the whale.
verses.singable:
Hurled after having extolled while in the palms of his hands;
Hurled like the swallowed extoller from the gut cavity.

title.urdu: پانچواں باب: حضورِ اقدس ﷺ کے معجزات کا ذکر

chapters.description: Focusing on the miracles performed by the Prophet ﷺ, such as the trees obeying his call and the clouds shading his journey.

verses.arabic:
جَاءَتْ لِدَعْوَتِهِ الأَشْجَارُ سَاجِدَةً
تَمْشِي إِلَيْهِ عَلَى سَاقٍ بِلاَ قَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Jāʾat li daʿwatihil ashjāru sājidatan
Tamshī ilayhi ʿalā sāqin bilā qadami
verses.english:
Trees came prostrating to him when he called,
Walking towards him on trunks without feet
verses.singable:
The trees had come for his call, prostrate in humility;
They came to him on their trunks, like footless legs, walking free.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّمَا سَطَرَتْ سَطْرًا لِمَا كَتَبَتْ
فُرُوعُهَا مِنْ بَدِيعِ الْخَطِّ بِاللَّقَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annamā saṭarat saṭran limā katabat
Furūʿuhā min badīʿil khaṭṭi bil laqami
verses.english:
As though they had inscribed lines of splendid calligraphy
With their branches along the path.
verses.singable:
As if they had drawn along the way a straight line for what
Their branches had written in the finest calligraphy.

verses.arabic:
مِثْلَ الغَمَامَةِ أَنَّى سَارَ سَائِرَةً
تَقِيهِ حَرَّ وَطِيسٍ لِلْهَجِيرِ حَمِي
verses.transliteration:
Mithlal ghamāmati annā sāra sāʾiratan
Taqīhi ḥarra waṭīsin lil hajīri ḥamī
verses.english:
Like the cloud that hovered over him wherever he went,
Shielding him from the intense oven of the midday heat.
verses.singable:
Just like the cloud moving with him as he moved anywhere;
From noon's intense oven heat protecting him constantly.

verses.arabic:
أَقْسَمْتُ بِالْقَمَرِ المُنْشَقِّ إِنَّ لَهُ
مِنْ قَلْبِهِ نِسْبَةً مَبْرُورَةَ القَسَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Aqsamtu bil qamaril munshaqqi inna lahu
Min qalbihi nisbatan mabrūratal qasami
verses.english:
I swear by the [Lord of the] moon that was split in two
a true oath: it has truly a connection with his heart.
verses.singable:
I swear an oath by the moon, which split in two, that it has
A likeness to his pure heart-an oath of veracity.

verses.arabic:
وَمَا حَوَى الغَارُ مِنْ خَيْرٍ وَمِنْ كَرَمِ
وَكُلُّ طَرْفٍ مِنَ الكُفَّارِ عَنْهُ عَمِي
verses.transliteration:
Wa mā ḥawal ghāru min khayrin wa min karamin
Wa kullu ṭarfin minal kuffāri ʿanhu ʿamī
verses.english:
And by the goodness and nobility contained in the cave
While the disbelievers’ every glance was blind to him.
verses.singable:
And what the cave did contain of good and nobility;
With every look from the disbelievers too blind to see.

verses.arabic:
فَالصِّدْقُ فِي الغَارِ وَالصِّدِّيقُ لَمْ يَرِمَا
وَهُمْ يَقُولُونَ مَا بِالْغَارِ مِنْ أَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Faṣ ṣidqu fīl ghāri waṣ ṣiddīqu lam yarimā
Wa hum yaqūlūna mā bil ghāri min arimi
verses.english:
Truthfulness and the True One in the cave wavered not
While they said ‘No one who breathes is in the cave!’
verses.singable:
The Truth and Truthful in Faith inside the cave hadn't moved;
All while they were saying that inside the cave none could be.

verses.arabic:
ظَنُّوا الحَمَامَ وَظَنُّوا الْعَنْكَبُوتَ عَلَى
خَيْرِ الْبَرِيَّةِ لَمْ تَنْسُجْ وَلَمْ تَحُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ẓannul ḥamāma wa ẓannul ʿankabūta ʿalā
Khayril bariyyati lam tansuj wa lam taḥumi
verses.english:
They supposed that a dove would never perch,
Or a spider would spin its web, for the Best of Creation
verses.singable:
They thought no dove hovered and no spider spun any web,
For him, the Best of Creation-thinking its vacancy.

verses.arabic:
وِقَايَةُ اللهِ أَغْنَتْ عَنْ مُضَاعَفَةٍ
مِنَ الدُّرُوعِ وَعَنْ عَالٍ مِنَ الأُطُمِ
verses.transliteration:
WiqāyatuLlāhi aghnat ʿan muḍāʿafatin
Minad durūʿi wa ʿan ʿālin minal uṭumi
verses.english:
Their protection by Allah absolved them of need
for additional armour or for lofty fortresses.
verses.singable:
Protection from God made needless reinforced armature,
Or towering citadels providing security.

verses.arabic:
مَا سَامَنِي الدَّهْرُ ضَيْمًا وَاسْتَجَرْتُ بِهِ
إِلاَّ وَنِلْتُ جِوَارًا مِنْهُ لَمْ يُضَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Mā sāmanid dahru ḍayman wastajartu bihi
Illā wa niltu jiwāran minhu lam yuḍami
verses.english:
Never, when fate oppressed me, have I sought refuge in him
but that I found sanctuary with him and was oppressed no more.
verses.singable:
No day has time gone to harm me and I have sought his care,
But that I have gotten care from him, without mockery.

verses.arabic:
وَلاَ الْتَمَسْتُ غِنَى الدَّارَيْنِ مِنْ يَدِهِ
إِلاَّ اسْتَلَمْتُ النَّدَى مِنْ خَيْرِ مُسْتَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa laltamastu ghinad dārayni min yadihi
Illāstalamtun nadā min khayri mustalami
verses.english:
And never have I sought wealth in both worlds from his hand
Without receiving largesse from the best of givers.
verses.singable:
Nor have I asked of the riches of both worlds from his hand,
Without largesse from the best of givers coming to me.

verses.arabic:
لاَ تُنْكِرِ الْوَحْيَ مِنْ رُؤْيَاهُ إِنَّ لَهُ
قَلْبًا إِذَا نَامَتِ العَيْنَانِ لَمْ يَنَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lā tunkiril waḥya min ruʾyāhu inna lahu
Qalban idhā nāmatil ʿaynāni lam yanami
verses.english:
Do not deny the revelations he received in his dreams;
For though his eyes slept, he had a heart that slept not.
verses.singable:
Do not deny revelation from his dreaming, because
His heart, though his eyes may shut, is open wide and does see.

verses.arabic:
وَذَاكَ حِينَ بُلُوغٍ مِنْ نُبَوَّتِهِ
فَلَيْسَ يُنْكَرُ فِيهِ حَالُ مُحْتَلِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa dhāka ḥīna bulūghin min nubuwwatihi
Fa laysa yunkaru fīhi ḥālu muḥtalimi
verses.english:
That was when he attained his Prophetic status;
the night dreams of mature men cannot be denied!
verses.singable:
And that was so at the very start of his prophethood:
So with that state of the dreaming no one can disagree.

verses.arabic:
تَبَارَكَ اللهُ مَا وَحَيٌ بِمُكْتَسَبٍ
وَلاَ نَبيُّ عَلَى غَيْبٍ بِمُتَّهَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Tabārak Allahu mā waḥyun bi muktasabin
Wa lā nabiyyun ʿalā ghaybin bi muttahami
verses.english:
Blessed is Allah! Revelation is not something acquired!
Nor can a Prophet’s knowledge of the Unseen be deemed suspect.
verses.singable:
How blest is God: revelation isn't something attained;
Nor is a prophet suspected in what we do not see.

verses.arabic:
كَمْ أَبْرَأَتْ وَصِبًا بِاللَّمْسِ رَاحَتُهُ
وَأَطْلَقَتْ أَرِبًا مِنْ رِبْقَةِ اللَّمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kam abraʾat waṣiban bil lamsi rāḥatuhu
Wa aṭlaqat ariban min ribqatil lamami
verses.english:
How many sick people were healed by the touch of his hand;
how many released from the tight knots of madness!
verses.singable:
How many times has his palm by touch relieved the unwell,
And freed the helpless from in the noose of insanity.

verses.arabic:
وَأَحْيَتِ السَّنَةَ الشَّهْبَاءَ دَعْوَتُهُ
حَتَّى حَكَتْ غُرَّةً فِي الأَعْصُرِ الدُّهُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa aḥyatis sanatash shahbāʾa daʿwatuhu
Ḥattā ḥakat ghurratan fīl aʿṣurid duhumi
verses.english:
His supplication brought life to a grey year [of drought] –
Marked it out from dark years like [a horse’s] white blaze.
verses.singable:
His call gave life to a gray and barren year full of drought;
Till it became like a star in ages of ebony;

verses.arabic:
بِعَارضٍ جَادَ أَوْ خِلْتَ البِطَاحَ بِهَا
سَيْبًا مِنَ اليَمِّ أَوْ سَيْلاً مِنَ العَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Bi ʿāriḍin jāda aw khiltal biṭāḥa bihā
Sayban minal yammi aw saylan minal ʿarimi
verses.english:
With generous rainclouds, so much that you would have thought
That the valleys flowed with sea water or the flood of ʿArim.
verses.singable:
With teeming clouds-you'd have thought the valleys had rivers flow
In from the sea, or a flooded dam that gushed viciously.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ السَّادِسُ فِي شَرَفِ الْقُرآنِ وَمَدْحِهِ
title.english: Chapter Six: The Nobility of the Qur’an and its Merit
title.urdu: چھٹا باب: قرآن مجید کی عظمت اور شرف کا بیان

chapters.description: Imam al-Busiri glorifies the Holy Qur'an, describing it as the ultimate everlasting miracle given to the Prophet ﷺ, surpassing all other scriptures.

verses.arabic:
دَعْنيِ وَوَصْفِي آيَاتٍ لَهُ ظَهَرَتْ
ظُهُورَ نَارِ القِرَى لَيْلاً عَلَى عَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Daʿnī wa waṣfī āyātin lahu ẓaharat
Ẓuhūra nāril qirā laylan ʿalā ʿalami
verses.english:
Let me describe to you the signs that were manifested for him,
Visible like the village beacons lit atop hills at night.
verses.singable:
Leave me alone to describe his miracles, which are plain

As nightly fire on mountains, lit as a courtesy.

verses.arabic:
فَالدُّرُّ يَزْدَادُ حُسْنًا وَهْوَ مُنْتَظِمٌ
وَلَيْسَ يَنْقُصُ قَدْرًا غَيْرَ مُنْتَظِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fad durru yazdādu ḥusnan wa huwa muntaẓimun
Wa laysa yanquṣu qadran ghayra muntaẓimi
verses.english:
Though a pearl is more lovely when strung [with others],
Its value is not diminished when alone and unstrung.
verses.singable:
For pearls increase in their beauty when arranged on a string,

But aren’t less precious kept alone and separately

verses.arabic:
فَمَا تَطَاوُلُ آمَالِ المَديحِ إِلَى
مَا فِيهِ مِنْ كَرَمِ الأَخْلاَقِ وَالشِّيَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Famā taṭāwulu āmālil madīḥi ilā
Mā fīhi min karamil akhlāqi wash shiyami
verses.english:
To what hope can the giver of praise aspire
Of doing justice to his noble qualities and traits?
verses.singable:
What eulogizing has any hope of stretching to reach

What he possesses in noble nature and quality?

verses.arabic:
آيَاتُ حَقٍّ مِنَ الرَّحْمَانِ مُحْدَثَةٌ
قَدِيمَةٌ صِفَةُ المَوْصُوفِ بِالقِدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Āyātu ḥaqqin minar raḥmāni muḥdathatun
Qadīmatun ṣifatul mawṣūfi bil qidami
verses.english:
Signs of truth from the All-Merciful—revealed within time
Yet pre-eternal; the attribute of Him who is pre-eternal.
verses.singable:
True signs from the Merciful, originated in time,
Beginningless, trait of Him Who is beginninglessly.

verses.arabic:
لَمْ تَقْتَرِنْ بِزَمِانٍ وَهْيَ تُخْبِرُنَا
عَنْ المَعَادِ وَعَنْ عَادٍ وَعَنْ إِرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lam taqtarin bizamānin wa hيya tukhbirunā
ʿAnil maʿādi wa ʿan ʿādin wa ʿan irami
verses.english:
They are not connected with time, yet they inform us
About the Resurrection, and about ʿĪd and Iram.
verses.singable:
They aren't bound by constraints of time, as they tell us of
The day to come, also Aad and Irum-past history.

verses.arabic:
دَامَتْ لَدَيْنَا فَفَاقَتْ كُلَّ مُعْجِزَةٍ
مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ إِذْ جَاءَتْ وَلَمْ تَدُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Dāmat ladaynā fafāqat kulla muʿjizatin
Minan nabiyyīna idh jāʾat wa lam tadumi
verses.english:
They remained with us, thus surpassing every miracle
Of the other Prophets, which came but did not last.
verses.singable:
They stayed among us, and so transcended all miracles
From all the prophets, as they had come and then ceased to be.

verses.arabic:
مُحْكَّمَاتٌ فَمَا تُبْقِيْنَ مِنْ شُبَهٍ
لِذِي شِقَاقٍ وَمَا تَبْغِيْنَ مِنْ حَكَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Muḥkamātun famā tubqīna min shubahin
Lidhī shiqāqin wa mā tabghīna min ḥakami
verses.english:
Unequivocal verses leaving no doubt to remain
in dissenters, and requiring no arbiter.
verses.singable:
So wise and clear, that they leave no room for a single doubt
To an opponent and have no need for a referee.

verses.arabic:
مَا حُورِبَتْ قَطُّ إِلاَّ عَادَ مِنْ حَرَبٍ
أَعْدَى الأَعَادِي إِلَيْهَا مُلْقِيَ السَّلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Mā ḥūribat qaṭṭu illā ʿāda min ḥarabin
Aʿdal aʿādī ilayhā mulqiyas salami
verses.english:
No implacable foe has attacked them
But that he retreated from battle and sued for peace.
verses.singable:
And never were they attacked except that from battle did
Return to them with surrendered arms the worst enemy.

verses.arabic:
رَدَّتْ بَلاَغَتُهَا دَعْوَى مُعَارِضِهَا
رَدَّ الغَيُورِ يَدَ الجَانِي عَنِ الْحُرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Raddat balāghatuhā daʿwā muʿāriḍihā
Raddal ghayūri yadal jānī ʿanil ḥurami
verses.english:
Their sheer eloquence refutes the claim of their opponent,
As a jealous man wards off an assailant’s hand from his wife.
verses.singable:
Their eloquence fought off their dissenters' claim, as a man
That is protective fights off attack from his family.

verses.arabic:
لَهَا مَعَانٍ كَمَوْجِ البَحْرِ فِي مَدَدٍ
وَفَوْقَ جَوْهَرِهِ فِي الْحُسْنِ وَالقِيَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lahā maʿānin ka mawjil baḥri fī madadin
Wa fawqa jawharihi fīl ḥusni wal qiyami
verses.english:
They contain meanings like the sea’s bountiful waves,
And surpass the sea’s jewels in beauty and value.
verses.singable:
Possessing meanings like waves upon the sea in support;
Surpassing its treasures in allure and sublimity.

verses.arabic:
فَمَا تُعَدُّ وَلاَ تُحْصَى عَجَائِبُهَا
وَلاَ تُسَامُ عَلَى الإِكْثَارِ بِالسَّأَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Famā tuʿaddu wa lā tuḥṣā ʿajāʾibuhā
Wa lā tusāmu ʿalal ikthāri bis saʾami
verses.english:
Their marvels can neither be counted nor numbered,
And frequent repetition never gives rise to tedium.
verses.singable:
Such that their wonders are countless and beyond any bound,
And never found to be tiresome in great quantity.

verses.arabic:
قَرَّتْ بِهَا عَيْنُ قَارِيْهَا فَقُلْتُ لَهُ
لَقَدْ ظَفِرْتَ بِحَبْلِ اللهِ فَاعْتَصِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Qarrat bihā ʿaynu qārīhā fa qultu lahu
Laqad ẓafirta bi ḥabliLlāhi faʿtaṣimi
verses.english:
They delighted the one who recited them, so I told him,
‘You have the Rope of Allah, so hold fast to it!’
verses.singable:
By them is soothed their reciter's eye, so I said to him,
"You have indeed found the rope of God, so hold steadily."

verses.arabic:
إِنْ تَتْلُهَا خِيفَةً مِنْ حَرَّ نَارِ لَظَى
أَطْفَأْتَ حَرِّ لَظَىَ مِنْ وِرْدِهَا الشَّبِمِ
verses.transliteration:
In tatluhā khīfatan min ḥarri nāri laẓā
Aṭfaʾta ḥarra laẓā min wirdihash shabimi
verses.english:
If you recite them out of fear of a blazing fire’s heat
from their cool wells they will quench the blazing heat
verses.singable:
If you recite them in worry of the fire of Hell,
From their cool fountain you douse the burn of Hell utterly.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّهَا الحَوْضُ تَبْيَضُّ الوُجُوهُ بِهِ
مِنَ العُصَاةِ وَقَدْ جَاءُوهُ كَالحُمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annahal ḥawḍu tabyaḍḍul wujūhu bihi
Minal ʿuṣāti wa qad jāʾūhu kal ḥumami
verses.english:
Like the Pool, they brighten the faces of the sinful,
Though they had come to it as black as charcoal.
verses.singable:
Just like the Basin, as it is whitening every face,
Of sinners coming to it like coals as black as can be.

verses.arabic:
وَكَالصِّرَاطِ وَكَالمِيزَانِ مَعْدِلَةً
فَالقِسْطُ مِنْ غَيْرِهَا فِي النَّاسِ لَمْ يَقُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa kaṣṣirāṭi wa kalmīzāni maʿdalatan
Falqisṭu min ghayrihā fin nāsi lam yaqumi
verses.english:
Like the Traverse and like Scales in justice;
True justice amongst men cannot be upheld from any other source.
verses.singable:
And like the straight Bridge and like the Balance in equity:
Without them no justice is maintained in society.

verses.arabic:
لاَ تَعْجَبَنْ لِحَسُودٍ رَاحَ يُنْكِرُهَا
تَجَاهُلاً وَهْوَ عَيْنُ الحَاذِقِ الفَهِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lā taʿjaban liḥasūdin rāḥa yunkiruhā
Tajāhulan wa huwa ʿaynul ḥādhiqil fahimi
verses.english:
Do not be amazed at an envious person who denies them
Feigning ignorance, though he has perception and understanding.
verses.singable:
Be not amazed by a hater in denial of them,
Who acts unknowing despite full knowledge and mastery.

verses.arabic:
قَدْ تُنْكِرُ الْعَيْنُ ضَوْءَ الشَّمْسِ مِنْ رَمَدٍ
وَيُنْكِرُ الفَمُ طَعْمَ المَاءِ مِنْ سَقَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Qad tunkirul ʿaynu ḍawʾash shamsi min ramadin
Wa yunkirul famu ṭaʿmal māʾi min saqami
verses.english:
For the eye, when inflamed, may be averse to the sun’s light;
And the mouth, when ailing, may loathe water’s taste.
verses.singable:
An eye may even reject the light of sun when inflamed;
A mouth rejecting the taste of water from malady.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ السَّابِعُ فِي إِسْرَائِهِ وَمِعْرَاجِهِ ﷺ
title.english: Chapter Seven: The Prophet’s Miraculous Night Journey and Celestial Ascension
title.urdu: ساتواں باب: حضورِ اقدس ﷺ کے اسرا اور معراج کا بیان

chapters.description: This chapter celebrates the night journey (Isra) and ascension (Mi'raj) of the Prophet ﷺ through the heavens to the Divine presence.

verses.arabic:
يَا خَيْرَ مَنْ يَمَّمَ العّافُونَ سَاحَتَهُ
سَعْيًا وَفَوْقَ مُتُونِ الأَيْنُقِ الرُّسُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā Khayra man yammamal ʿāfūna sāḥatahu
Saʿyan wa fawqa mutūnil aynuqir rusumi
verses.english:
O best of those to whose courtyard seekers turn to
Hastening on foot or the backs of strong camels!
verses.singable:
O best of those whose front yard is sought by those seeking good,
On foot and on backs of camels treading vigorously!

verses.arabic:
وَمَنْ هُوَ الآيَةُ الكُبْرَى لِمُعْتَبِرٍ
وَمَنْ هُوَ النِّعْمَةُ العُظْمَى لِمُغْتَنِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa man huwal āyatul kubrā limuʿtabirin
Wa man huwan niʿmatul ʿuẓmā limughtanimi
verses.english:
O Greatest Sign for those who contemplate!
O Supreme Grace for those who seek to gain!
verses.singable:
And you who are sign supreme for one who does seek to know!
And greatest blessing for one in search of prosperity!

verses.arabic:
سَرَيْتَ مِنْ حَرَمٍ لَيْلاً إِلَى حَرَمٍ
كَمَا سَرَى البَدْرُ فِي دَاجٍ مِنَ الظُّلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Sarayta min ḥaramin laylan ilā ḥaramin
Kamā saral badru fī dājin minaẓ ẓulami
verses.english:
You journeyed by night, from Sanctuary to Sanctuary
as the full moon travels through the pitch black sky.
verses.singable:
From sanctum to sanctum you had made the trip overnight;
Just as the moon makes the trip through darkness nocturnally.

verses.arabic:
وَبِتَّ تَرْقَى إِلَى أَنْ نِلْتَ مَنْزِلَةً
مِنْ قَابِ قَوْسَيْنِ لَمْ تُدْرَكْ وَلَمْ تُرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa bitta tarqā ilā an nilta manzilatan
Min qābi qawsayni lam tudrak wa lam turami
verses.english:
That night you ascended till you reached a station
Of Two Bows’ Length—one never attained or hoped for!
verses.singable:
You spent the night rising up until you had reached a place
A length of two bows, not gained or sought in all history.

verses.arabic:
وَقَدَّمَتْكَ جَمِيعُ الأَنْبِيَاءِ بِهَا
وَالرُّسْلِ تَقْدِيمَ مَخْدُومٍ عَلَى خَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa qaddamatka jamīʿul anbiyāʾi bihā
War rusli taqdīma makhdūmin ʿalā khadami
verses.english:
There all the Prophets and Emissaries gave you precedence,
As a master is given precedence by those who serve him.
verses.singable:
And all the prophets and messengers had put you ahead,
Just as the served over servants has the priority.

verses.arabic:
وَأَنْتَ تَخْتَرِقُ السَّبْعَ الطِّبَاقَ بِهِمْ
فِي مَوْكِبٍ كُنْتَ فِيهِ الصَّاحِبَ العَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa anta takhtariqus sabʿaṭ ṭibāqa bihim
Fī mawkibin kunta fīhi ṣāhibal ʿalami
verses.english:
You traversed the seven heavens with them [behind you]
In a procession wherein you were the standard-bearer.
verses.singable:
As you proceeded through all the seven levels with them,
In a procession, you were the banner bearer to see;

verses.arabic:
حَتَّى إِذَا لَمْ تَدَعْ شَأْوًا لِمُسْتَبِقٍ
مِنَ الدُّنُوِّ وَلاَ مَرْقَى لِمُسْتَنِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ḥattā idhā lam tadaʿ shaʾwan limustabiqin
Minad dunuwwi wa lā marqan li mustanimi
verses.english:
Until your proximity left no space for the avid seeker,
Nor any higher summit for one seeking elevation.
verses.singable:
Until you had left no goal for the ambitious in hope
Of closeness, nor height for someone seeking ascendency.

verses.arabic:
خَفَضْتَ كُلَّ مَقَامٍ بِالإِضَافَةٍ إِذْ
نُودِيْتَ بِالرَّفْعِ مِثْلَ المُفْرَدِ العَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Khafaḍta kulla maqāmin bil iḍāfati idh
Nūdīta bir rafʿi mithlal mufradil ʿalami
verses.english:
You surpassed all other stations in comparison
When acclaimed on high as ‘The Matchless and Eminent’–
verses.singable:
You humbled all ranks by annexation: you were addressed,
Like unannexed proper noun, with singular dignity.

verses.arabic:
كَيْمَا تَفُوزَ بِوَصْلٍ أَيِّ مُسْتَتِرٍ
عَنْ العُيُونِ وَسِرٍّ أَيِّ مُكْتَتَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kaymā tafūza biwaṣlin ayyi mustatirin
ʿAnil ʿuyūni wa sirrin ayyi muktatami
verses.english:
So that you might triumph through a tryst hidden
From sight. How hidden a secret that was!
verses.singable:
So that you triumph with a connection oh so concealed
From eyes, and a secret oh so shrouded in mystery!

verses.arabic:
فَحُزْتَ كُلَّ فَخَارٍ غَيْرَ مُشْتَرَكٍ
وَجُزْتَ كُلَّ مَقَامٍ غَيْرَ مُزْدَحَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa ḥuzta kulla fakhārin ghayra mushtarakin
Wa juzta kulla maqāmin ghayra muzdaḥami
verses.english:
So you received every glory without rival
And gained every station alone, unbeset.
verses.singable:
So you obtained every glory, no one else joining you;
And you surpassed all alone, unchallenged, every degree.

verses.arabic:
وَجَلَّ مِقْدَارُ مَا وُلِّيتَ مِنْ رُتَبٍ
وَعَزَّ إِدْرَاكُ مَا أُولِيْتَ مِنْ نِعَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa jalla miqdāru wā wullīta min rutabin
Wa ʿazza idrāku mā ūlīta min niʿami
verses.english:
How glorious is the worth of the ranks you were given
How difficult it is to grasp the graces conferred!
verses.singable:
The measure of stations you were granted, what majesty!
Attaining the blessings you were given, what rarity!

verses.arabic:
بُشْرَى لَنَا مَعْشَرَ الإِسْلَامِ إِنَّ لَنَا
مِنَ العِنَايَةِ رُكْنًا غَيْرَ مُنْهَدِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Bushrā lanā maʿsharal islāmi inna lanā
Minal ʿināyati ruknan ghayra munhadimi
verses.english:
Glad tidings for us, O people of Islam,
For we have an indestructible pillar of divine care.
verses.singable:
Great news for us, people of Islam! Indeed, we possess
A pillar of special care erected unbreakably.

verses.arabic:
لَمَّا دَعَا اللهُ دَاعِينَا لِطَاعَتِهِ
بِأَكْرَمِ الرُّسْلِ كُنَّا أَكْرَمَ الأُمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Lammā daʿāLlāhu dāʿīnā liṭāʿatihi
Bi akramir rusli kunna akramal umami
verses.english:
When Allah named the one who called us to obey Him
The Noblest of Emissaries, we became the noblest of nations!
verses.singable:
And since Allah called the one who called us to serving Him
The Greatest Prophet, we are the greatest community.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ الثَّامِنُ فِي جِهَادِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ
title.urdu: آٹھواں باب: حضورِ اقدس ﷺ کے جہاد کا بیان

chapters.description: Depicting the valor, strategic wisdom, and spiritual orientation of the battles fought by the Prophet ﷺ and his companions to defend Islam.

verses.arabic:
رَاعَتْ قُلُوبَ الْعِدَا أَنْبَاءُ بِعْثَتِهِ
كَنَبْأَةٍ أَجْفَلَتْ غُفْلاً مِنَ الْغَنَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Rāʿat qulūbal ʿidā anbāʾu biʿthatihi
Kanabʾatin ajfalat ghuflan minal ghanami
verses.english:
The news of his being sent had cast fear into the hearts of foes
as heedless sheep are startled by sudden noise.
verses.singable:
The news of his being sent alarmed the hearts of the foes,
Just like a roar causing heedless sheep to startle and flee.

verses.arabic:
مَا زَالَ يَلْقَاهُمُ فِي كُلِّ مُعْتَرَكٍ
حَتَّى حَكَوْا بِالْقَنَا لَحْمًا عَلَى وَضَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Mā zāla yalqāhumu fī kulli muʿtarakin
Ḥattā ḥakaw bil qanā laḥman ʿalā waḍami
verses.english:
He continued to meet them on every battleground
Until spears cut them, like meat on a butcher’s block.
verses.singable:
He kept courageously facing them at each battlefield,
Till they were butchered by spears like meat in a butchery.

verses.arabic:
وَدُّوا الفِرَارَ فَكَادُوا يَغْبِطُونَ بِهِ
أَشْلاَءَ شَالَتْ مَعَ الْعِقْبَانِ وَالرَّخَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Waddul firāra fakādū yaghbiṭūna bihi
Ashlāʾa shālat maʿal ʿiqbāni war rakhami
verses.english:
They longed to flee, almost jealous of
The dead flesh carried off by hawks and vultures.
verses.singable:
They longed to flee so much that they watched the parts carried off
By buzzards and vultures in a state of near jealousy.

verses.arabic:
تَمْضِي اللَّيَالِي وَلاَ يَدْرُونَ عِدَّتَهَا
مَا لَمْ تَكُنْ مِنْ لَيَالِي الأَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Tamḍil layālī wa lā yadrūna ʿiddatahā
Mā lam takun min layālil ash-huril ḥurumi
verses.english:
The nights passed with them unaware of their number,
Apart from the nights of the Sacred Months.
verses.singable:
The nights would pass with them unaware the number of them,
Except the nights of the Sacred Months of tranquility.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّمَا الدِّينُ ضَيْفٌ حَلَّ سَاحَتَهُمْ
بِكُلِّ قَرْمٍ إِلَى لَحْمِ العِدَا قَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annamad dīnu ḍayfun ḥalla sāḥatahum
Bi kulli qarmin ilā laḥmil ʿidā qarimi
verses.english:
As if the religion were a guest that had arrived at their courtyard,
With every honoured chieftain eager for the flesh of their foes.
verses.singable:
As if religion had been a guest arrived at their yard,
With every nobleman craving meat of the enemy.

verses.arabic:
يَجُرُّ بَحْرَ خَمِيسٍ فَوْقَ سَابِحَةٍ
يَرْمِى بِمَوْجٍ مِنَ الأَبْطَالِ مُلْتَطِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yajurru baḥra khamīsin fawqa sābiḥatin
Yarmī bi mawjin minal abṭāli multaṭimi
verses.english:
Bringing forth a sea of soldiers on swift steeds,
Tossing wave upon wave of onrushing heroes.
verses.singable:
It brought an ocean of troops on gracefully-floating steeds,
Advancing waves made of heroes, surging successively.

verses.arabic:
مِنْ كُلِّ مُنْتَدَبٍ لِلهِ مُحْتَسِبٍ
يَسْطُو بِمُسْتَأْصِلٍ لِلكُفْرِ مُصْطَلِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Min kulli muntadibin liLlāhi muḥtasibin
Yasṭū bimustaʾṣilin lil kufri muṣṭalimi
verses.english:
All responding to Allah’s call and anticipating reward,
Boldly charging on, and uprooting disbelief.
verses.singable:
Each in response to the call of God, in hope of reward,
Attacking armed to uproot and shatter idolatry.

verses.arabic:
حَتىَّ غَدَتْ مِلَّةُ الإِسْلاَمِ وَهْيَ بِهِمْ
مِنْ بَعْدِ غُرْبَتِهَا مَوْصُولَةَ الرَّحِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ḥatta ghadat millatul islāmi wahya bihim
Min baʿdi ghurbatihā mawṣūlatar rahimi
verses.english:
Until the religion of Islam was, through them,
Reunited in kinship after being in exile.
verses.singable:
Until the Faith of Islam, with them among it, became-
Once having been foreign-now a unified family.

verses.arabic:
مَكْفُولَةً أَبَدًا مِنْهُمْ بِخَيْرِ أَبٍ
وَخَيْرِ بَعْلٍ فَلَمْ تَيْتَمْ وَلَمْ تَئِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Makfūlatan abadan minhum bi khayri abin
Wa khayri baʿlin fa lam taytam wa lam taʾimi
verses.english:
Forever protected from [foes] by the best of fathers and husbands,
So that she was no longer an orphan or a widow.
verses.singable:
Protected forever from them by the best father and
Best husband, so not an orphan or a widow is she.

verses.arabic:
هُمُ الجِبَالُ فَسَلْ عَنْهُمْ مُصَادِمَهُمْ
مَاذَا رَأَى مِنْهُمُ فِي كُلِّ مُصْطَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Humul jibālu fasal ʿanhum muṣādimahum
Mādhā raʾā minhumu fī kulli muṣṭadami
verses.english:
They were mountains! Ask those who came to blows with them
What they saw from them on every field of battle.
verses.singable:
They are the mountains, so ask about them their battle-foe,
At every battle, the things from them that he used to see.

verses.arabic:
وَسَلْ حُنَيْنًا وَسَلْ بَدْرًا وَسَلْ أُحُدًا
فُصُولَ حَتْفٍ لَهُمْ أَدْهَى مِنَ الوَخَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa sal ḥunaynan wa sal badran wa sal uḥudan
Fuṣūla ḥatfin lahum adʾhā minal wakhami
verses.english:
Ask Ḥunayn, ask Badr, ask Uḥud—
Seasons of death, more disastrous for them than the plague!
verses.singable:
And ask Hunayn, question Badr, and even ask Uhud, too-
Events of death for them, worse than plague in catastrophe.

verses.arabic:
المُصْدِرِي البِيضِ حُمْرًا بَعْدَ مَا وَرَدَتْ
مِنَ العِدَا كُلَّ مُسْوَدٍّ مِنَ اللِّمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Almuṣdiril bīḍi ḥumran baʿda mā waradat
Minal ʿidā kulla muswaddin minal limami
verses.english:
White swords returned, dripping red after taking drink
From beneath the black heads of all their enemies.
verses.singable:
Returning white blades now turned to crimson after they reached
The fighters with flowing hair of black from the enemy.

verses.arabic:
وَالكَاتِبِينَ بِسُمْرِ الخَطِّ مَا تَرَكَتْ
أَقْلاَمُهُمْ حَرْفَ جِسْمٍ غَيْرَ مُنَعَجِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wal kātibīna bi sumril khaṭṭi mā tarakat
Aqlāmuhum ḥarfa jismin ghayra munʿajimi
verses.english:
Like scribes with wielded spears for pens,
Leaving no letter of a body unmarked with dots.
verses.singable:
Inscribing with spears of Lettering, their pens didn't leave
A body line with undotted i, and crossed every t.

verses.arabic:
شَاكِي السِّلاَحِ لَهُمْ سِيمَا تُمَيِّزُهُمْ
وَالوَرْدُ يَمْتَازُ بِالسِّيمَا عَنِ السَّلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Shākis silāḥi lahum sīmā tumayyizuhum
Wal wardu yamtāzu bis sīmā ʿanis salami
verses.english:
Well-armed, they were distinguished by a clear sign,
as a rose differs from an acacia tree in its features.
verses.singable:
With sharpened weapons they had a mark to set them apart;
A rose is set by its mark apart from a thorny tree.

verses.arabic:
تُهْدِي إِلَيْكَ رِيَاحُ النَّصْرِ نَشْرَهُمُ
فَتَحْسَبُ الزَّهْرَ فِي الأَكْمَامِ كُلَّ كَمِي
verses.transliteration:
Tuhdī ilayka riyāḥun naṣri nashrahumu
Fataḥsabuz zahra fīl akmāmi kulla kamī
verses.english:
The winds of victory bring you their scent as a gift;
You would think each armed hero a flower in bud!
verses.singable:
The victory winds convey to you the sweet news of them;
So flowers in bloom you'd reckon every soldier to be.

verses.arabic:
كَأَنَّهُمْ فِي ظُهُورِ الخَيْلِ نَبْتُ رُبًا
مِنْ شِدَّةِ الحَزْمِ لاَ مِنْ شِدَّةِ الحُزُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ka annahum fī ẓuhūril khayli nabtu ruban
Min shiddatil ḥazmi lā min shiddatil ḥuzumi
verses.english:
As if, riding their steeds, they were flowers on a hill-top
Held firm not by strong stirrups, but by strength of resolve.
verses.singable:
They were in horseback like firmly-rooted plants up on hills-
From grit so solid, not girth of saddle held solidly.

verses.arabic:
طَارَتْ قُلُوبُ العِدَا مِنْ بَأْسِهِمْ فَرَقًا
فَمَا تُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ البَهْمِ وَالبُهُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ṭārat qulūbul ʿidā min baʾsihim faraqan
Famā tufarriqu baynal bahmi wal buhami
verses.english:
Their forceful onslaught so put their foes’ hearts to flight
that you could not have told a herd from a warrior.
verses.singable:
The enemies' hearts were fluttering in fear of their force,
Unable to tell a lamb apart from brave cavalry.

verses.arabic:
وَمَنْ تَكُنْ بِرَسُولِ اللهِ نُصْرَتُهُ
إِنْ تَلْقَهُ الأُسْدُ فِي آجَامِهَا تَجِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa man takun birasūliLlāhi nuṣratuhu
In talqahul usdu fī ājāmihā tajimi
verses.english:
For the one whose help comes from the Emissary of Allah
Even lions finding him in their dens will be loth to face him.
verses.singable:
Whoever is aided by the Messenger of Allah,
If lions meet him inside their woods, they bow silently.

verses.arabic:
وَلَنْ تَرَى مِنْ وَليٍّ غَيْرِ مُنْتَصِرٍ
بِهِ وَلاَ مِنْ عَدُوٍّ غَيْرِ مُنْقَصِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lan tarā min waliyyin ghayri muntaṣirin
Bihi wa lā min ʿaduwwin ghayri munqasimi
verses.english:
You will not see one saint who is not victorious through him;
Or a single foe of his who is not dealt mortal blows.
verses.singable:
You will not see any saint that isn't victorious
Through him, nor any opponent not destroyed utterly.

verses.arabic:
أَحَلَّ أُمَّتَهُ فِي حِرْزِ مِلَّتِهِ
كَاللَّيْثِ حَلَّ مَعَ الأَشْبَالِ فِي أَجَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Aḥalla ummatahu fī ḥirzi millatihi
Kal laythi ḥalla maʿal ashbāli fī ajami
verses.english:
He gave his nation sanctuary in the stronghold of his faith,
As a lion settles in with its cubs in a thicket.
verses.singable:
He put his nation to dwell inside the fort of his faith;
Like lion dwelling inside the forest with progeny.

verses.arabic:
كَمْ جَدَّلَتْ كَلِمَاتُ اللهِ مِنْ جَدَلٍ
فِيهِ وَكَمْ خَصَمَ البُرْهَانُ مِنْ خَصِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kam jaddalat kalimātuLlāhi min jadilin
Fīhi wa kam khaṣamal burhānu min khaṣimi
verses.english:
How often have Allah’s words felled his opponents,
And how often the Qur’an has defeated its enemies!
verses.singable:
How often the words of God refuted the quarrelsome
About him! How often proof defeated the enemy!

verses.arabic:
كَفَاكَ بِالْعِلْمِ فِي الأُمِّيِّ مُعْجِزَةً
فِي الجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَالتَّأْدِيبِ فِي اليُتُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Kafāka bil ʿilmi fīl ummiyyi muʿjizatan
Fīl jāhiliyyati wat taʾdībi fīl yutumi
verses.english:
The erudition of an unlettered one is miracle enough
in an Age of Ignorance, as is an orphan’s education.
verses.singable:
Enlightenment in the one unread is wonder enough,
In Age of Darkness; and good upbringing in orphancy.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ التَّاسِعُ فِي تَوَسُّلِ بِرَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺChapter Nine: Seeking Intercession through the Prophet ﷺ
title.english: Chapter Nine: Seeking Intercession through the Prophet ﷺ
title.urdu: نواں باب: شفاعت اور توسل کا ذکر

chapters.description: In this chapter, the poet turns inward, confessing his shortcomings and requesting the Prophet's ﷺ intercession on the Day of Judgment.

verses.arabic:
خَدَمْتُهُ بِمَدِيحٍ أَسْتَقِيلُ بِهِ
ذُنُوبَ عُمْرٍ مَضَى فِي الشِّعْرِ وَالخِدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Khadamtuhu bi madīḥin astaqīlu bihi
Dhunūba ʿumrin maḍā fish shiʿri wal khidami
verses.english:
By this eulogy I have served him, seeking absolution
For the sins of a life spent in poetry and patronage.
verses.singable:
I've served him with eulogy by which I seek to erase
The sins of life spent in poem and in servility.

verses.arabic:
إِذْ قَلَّدَانِيَ مَا تُخْشَى عَوَاقِبُهُ
كَأَنَّنِي بِهِمَا هَدْىٌ مِنَ النَّعَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Idh qalladāniya mā tukhshā ʿawāqibuhu
Ka annanī bihimā hadyun minan naʿami
verses.english:
For these two have yoked me, with consequences I fear;
as if I were, on their account, a ritual lamb for slaughter.
verses.singable:
For they have led me to things of horrible aftermath;
Akin to livestock decreed by them for the butchery.

verses.arabic:
أَطَعْتُ غَيَّ الصِّبَا فِي الحَالَتَيْنِ وَمَا
حَصَلْتُ إِلاَّ عَلَى الآثَامِ وَالنَّدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Aṭaʿtu ghayyaṣ ṣibā fīl ḥālatayni wa mā
Ḥaṣaltu illā ʿalal āthāmi wan nadami
verses.english:
In both I obeyed the reckless folly of youth,
And gained nothing except sins and regrets.
verses.singable:
I have obeyed the deceit of youth in both cases and
Got nothing but sins and then regret, oh, what misery!

verses.arabic:
فَيَا خَسَارَةَ نَفْسٍ فِي تِجَارَتِهَا
لَمْ تَشْتَرِ الدِّينَ بِالدُّنْيَا وَلَمْ تَسُمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fayā khasārata nafsin fī tijāratihā
Lam tashtarid dīna bid dunyā wal lam tasumi
verses.english:
Alas, what abject loss for a soul in its transaction!
It did not buy the Next World at the price of this – or even try to!
verses.singable:
Oh, what a loss for my soul, the awful deal that it made!
Not buying faith with this world, not even browsing to see.

verses.arabic:
وَمَنْ يَبِعْ آجِلاً مِنْهُ بِعَاجِلِهِ
يَبِنْ لَهُ الْغَبْنُ فِي بَيْعٍ وَفِي سَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa man yabiʿ ājilan minhu bi ʿājilihi
Yabin lahul ghabnu fī bayʿin wa fī salami
verses.english:
Anyone who sells the Hereafter for the ephemeral
Will be cheated in their present and future transactions.
verses.singable:
Whoever sells off his future for his present will come
To see the loss in his sale and future delivery.

verses.arabic:
إِنْ آتِ ذَنْبًا فَمَا عَهْدِي بِمُنْتَقِضٍ
مَنَ النَّبِيِّ وَلاَ حَبْلِي بِمُنْصَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
In āti dhanban famā ʿahdī bimuntaqiḍin
Minan nabiyyi wa lā ḥablī bimunṣarimi
verses.english:
I have sinned, yet my pledge to the Prophet is unbroken;
Nor has the rope binding me to him been severed.
verses.singable:
If I engage in a sin, my covenant isn't void
With him, the Prophet; nor is the rope dissevered from me.

verses.arabic:
فَإِنَّ لِي ذِمَّةً مِنْهُ بِتَسْمِيَتيِ
مُحَمَّداً وَهْوَ أَوْفَى الخَلْقِ بِالذِّمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa inna lī dhimmatan minhu bi tasmiyatī
Muḥammadan wa huwa awfāl khalqi bidh dhimami
verses.english:
For I have a protective covenant with him owing to my name—
Muḥammad—and he is the most loyal of mankind to covenants.
verses.singable:
By naming my son Muhammad I am in pledge to him;
And none is more faithful in fulfilling pledges than he.

verses.arabic:
إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ فِي مَعَادِي آخِذًا بِيَدِي
فَضْلاً وَإِلاَّ فَقُلْ يَا زَلَّةَ القَدَمِ
verses.transliteration:
In lam yakun fī maʿādī ākhidhan bi yadī
Faḍlan wa illā faqul yā zallatal qadami
verses.english:
If, in the Hereafter, he does not take me by the hand
From pure grace, what a terrible slip of the foot!
verses.singable:
At my appointment, if he's not holding onto my hand-
In graciousness-say, "Oh, what a fall into tragedy!"

verses.arabic:
حَاشَاهُ أَنْ يَحْرِمَ الرَّاجِي مَكَارِمَهُ
أَوْ يَرْجِعَ الجَارُ مِنْهُ غَيْرَ مُحْتَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Ḥāshāhu an yaḥrimar rājī makārimahu
Aw yarjiʿal jāru minhu ghayra muḥtarami
verses.english:
Far be it for him to deprive one hopeful of his generous gifts,
Or for a neighbour to turn back without being honoured by him!
verses.singable:
Far be it from him to bar the aspirant of his gifts,
Or send away in dishonor from him a refugee.

verses.arabic:
وَمُنْذُ أَلْزَمْتُ أَفْكَارِي مَدَائِحَهُ
وَجَدْتُهُ لِخَلاَصِي خَيْرَ مُلْتَزِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa mundhu alzamtu afkārī madāʾiḥahu
Wajadtuhu likhalāṣī khayra multazimi
verses.english:
Ever since I have focused my thoughts on his praises,
I have found him the one most committed to my salvation.
verses.singable:
And since committing my thoughts to singing praises of him,
I've found him to be the most committed to saving me.

verses.arabic:
وَلَنْ يَفُوتَ الغِنَى مِنْهُ يَدًا تَرِبَتْ
إِنَّ الحَيَا يُنْبِتَ الأَزْهَارَ فِي الأَكَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lan yafūtal ghinā minhu yadan taribat
Innal ḥayā yunbitul azhāra fīl akami
verses.english:
His wealth will not overlook a dusty, needy hand;
For showers cause flowers to bloom on hill-tops.
verses.singable:
The riches from him will not neglect a poor, dusty hand;
Indeed, the rain causes even hills to be flowery.

verses.arabic:
وَلَمْ أُرِدْ زَهْرَةَ الدُّنْيَا الَّتيِ اقْتَطَفَتْ
يَدَا زُهَيْرٍ بِمَا أَثْنَى عَلَى هَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lam urid zahratad dunyal latiqtaṭafat
Yadā zuhayrin bimā athnā ʿalā harimi
verses.english:
I sought not the flowers of this world
Which Zuhayr picked for his praise of Harim.
verses.singable:
I sought no bloom of the lower world the hands of Zuhayr
Had picked for having presented Herim with flattery.

title.arabic: الْفَصْلُ الْعَاشِرُ فِي الْمُنَاجَاةِ وَعَرْضِ الْحَاجَاتِ
title.english: Chapter Ten: Intimate Discourse and the Petition of Needs
title.urdu: دسواں باب: مناجات اور بارگاہِ رب العزت میں التجا

chapters.description: The final chapter is an intimate supplication, praying to Allah for forgiveness, mercy, and peace, through the blessing of the Prophet ﷺ.

verses.arabic:
يَا أَكْرَمَ الخَلْقِ مَالَي مَنْ أَلُوذُ بِهِ
سِوَاكَ عِنْدَ حُلُولِ الحَادِثِ العَمِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā akramal khalqi mālī man alūdhu bihi
Siwāka ʿinda ḥulūlil ḥādithil ʿamimi
verses.english:
O Noblest of Creation, whose protection can I seek
But yours, when the Universal Event comes to pass?
verses.singable:
Most Noble of All Creation, what refuge do I have
But you at the coming of the global emergency?

verses.arabic:
وَلَنْ يَضِيقَ رَسُولَ اللهِ جَاهُكَ بيِ
إِذَا الكَرِيمُ تَجَلَّى بِاسْمِ مُنْتَقِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa lan yaḍīqa rasūlaLlāhi jāhuka bī
Idhal karīmu tajjalā bismi muntaqimi
verses.english:
O Emissary of Allah, your rank will not be diminished
By me when the Generous One appears, named as the Avenger.
verses.singable:
O Messenger of Allah, your rank won't shrink from me when
The Generous manifests His punishing quality.

verses.arabic:
فَإِنَّ مِنْ جُودِكَ الدُّنْيَا وَضَرَّتَهَا
وَمِنْ عُلُومِكَ عِلْمَ اللَّوْحِ وَالقَلَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Fa inna min jūdikad dunyā wa ḍarratahā
Wa min ʿulūmika ʿilmal lawḥi wal qalami
verses.english:
For this world and its companion the Next come from your bounty,
And part of what you know is knowledge of the Tablet and Pen.
verses.singable:
Yes, from your grace is indeed the world as well as its mate;
And of your knowledge the Tablet and the Pen of decree.

verses.arabic:
يَا نَفْسُ لاَ تَقْنَطِي مِنْ زَلَّةٍ عَظُمَتْ
إَنَّ الكَبَائِرَ فِي الغُفْرَانِ كَاللَّمَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā nafsu lā taqnaṭī min zallatin ʿaẓumat
Innal kabāʾira fīl ghufrāni kal lamami
verses.english:
O soul, despair not over a fault that is immense;
Enormities, with divine forgiveness, are like minor errors.
verses.singable:
O soul, despair not because of a mistake that is grave;
Enormities are like slips compared to His clemency.

verses.arabic:
لَعَلَّ رَحْمَةَ رَبِّي حِينَ يَقْسِمُهَا
تَأْتِي عَلَى حَسَبِ العِصْيَانِ فِي الْقِسَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Laʿalla raḥmata rabbī ḥīna yaqsimuhā
Taʾtī ʿalā ḥasabil ʿiṣyāni fīl qisami
verses.english:
Perchance my Lord’s mercy, when He apportions it,
Will be distributed in accordance with the measure of sins.
verses.singable:
And hopefully, mercy from my Lord when He gives it out
Will come according to sinfulness in its quantity.

verses.arabic:
يَا رَبِّ وَاجْعَلْ رَجَائِي غَيْرَ مُنْعَكِسٍ
لَدَيْكَ وَاجْعَلْ حِسَابِي غَيْرَ مُنْخَرِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā rabbi wajʿal rajāʾī ghayra munʿakisin
Ladayka wajʿal ḥisābī ghayra munkharimi
verses.english:
O my Lord, let not my hope in You be overturned
And do not make my account devoid of value.
verses.singable:
My Lord, and make not my hope a hope that is overturned
With you; and make my expectance with no deficiency.

verses.arabic:
وَالْطُفْ بِعَبْدِكَ فِي الدَّارَيْنِ إَنَّ لَهُ
صَبْرًا مَتَى تَدْعُهُ الأَهْوَالُ يَنْهَزِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Walṭuf biʿabdika fid dārayni inna lahu
Ṣabran matā tadʿuhul ahwālu yanhazimi
verses.english:
Be gentle with Your servant in this life and the Next;
For his patience flees when he is visited by terrors.
verses.singable:
Be kind to Your slave in both abodes; for his fortitude
Whenever terrors call out to it will turn tail and flee.

verses.arabic:
وَأْذَنْ لِسُحْبِ صَلاَةٍ مِنْكَ دَائِمَةٍ
عَلَى النَّبِيِّ بِمُنْهَلٍّ وَمُنْسَجِمِ
verses.transliteration:
Waʾdhan lisuḥbi ṣalātin minka dāʾimatin
ʿAlan nabiyyi bimunhallin wa munsajimi
verses.english:
And may clouds of Your blessings rain down constantly
Upon the Prophet, pouring down in abundance.
verses.singable:
Let clouds of blessing from You, unending, rain down upon
The Prophet, with pouring rain so heavily, steadily.

verses.arabic:
مَا رَنَّحَتْ عَذَبَاتِ الْبَانِ رِيحُ صَبًا
وَأَطْرَبَ الْعِيسَ حَادِي الْعِيسِ بِالنَّغَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Mā rannaḥat ʿadhabātil bāni rīḥu ṣaban
Wa aṭrabal ʿīsa ḥādil ʿīsi bin naghami
verses.english:
For as long as the east wind stirs the branches of willows,
And camel-drivers bring joy to their camels with melody.
verses.singable:
For longer than willow branches by the east wind are swayed;
And camel drivers excite the camels with melody.

verses.arabic:
ثُمَّ الرِّضَا عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ وَعَنْ عُمَرٍ
وَعَنْ عَلِيٍّ وَعَنْ عُثْمَانَ ذِي الْكَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Thummar riḍā ʿan abī bakrin wa ʿan ʿumarin
Wa ʿan ʿaliyyin wa ʿan ʿuthmāna dhil karami
verses.english:
Then extend Your good-pleasure to Abū Bakr and to ʿUmar
And to ‘Ali, and to ʿUthmān, the generous
verses.singable:
Then pleasure with Abu Bakr, the greatest of company,
And Umar, bearer of Truth, and then Uthman, and Ali,

verses.arabic:
وَالآلِ وَالصَّحْبِ ثُمَّ التَابِعِيَن فَهُمْ
أَهْلُ التُّقَى وَالنَّقَا وَالحِلْمِ وَالْكَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wal āli waṣ ṣaḥbi thummat tābiʿīna fahum
Ahlut tuqā wan naqā wal ḥilmi wal karami
verses.english:
And to the Family and the Companions, and then the Followers—the people of Godfearingness,
purity, forbearance and generosity.
verses.singable:
The Family and Companions and all the Followers,
The people of purity and patience and piety.

verses.arabic:
يِا رَبِّ بِالمُصْطَفَى بَلِّغْ مَقَاصِدَنَا
وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا مَا مَضَى يَا وَاسِعَ الكَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Yā rabbi bil muṣṭafā balligh maqāṣidanā
Waghfir lanā mā maḍā yā wāsiʿal karami
verses.english:
O Lord, by the Chosen One, allow us to attain our hopes
And forgive us for what has gone past, O Vastly Generous.
verses.singable:
My Lord, by the Chosen One, make our hopes come to be,
And pardon us what has gone, O Vast in Gen'rosity.

verses.arabic:
وَاغْفِرْ إِلَهِي لِكُلِ المُسْلِمِينَ بِمَا
يَتْلُونَ فِي المَسْجِدِ الأَقْصَى وَفِي الحَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Waghfir ilāhī li kullil muslimīna bimā
Yatluna fīl masjidil aqṣā wa fīl ḥarami
verses.english:
And forgive, O God, all of the Muslims, by virtue
Of what they recite in the Furthest Mosque [al-Aqṣā] and the Sacrosanct Mosque [al-Ḥaram]
verses.singable:
And please, my God, do forgive all of the Muslims by what
They all recite at the Holy Mosque and the Sanctity.

verses.arabic:
بِجَاهِ مَنْ بَيْتُهُ فِي طَيْبَةٍ حَرَمٌ
وَاسْمُهُ قَسَمٌ مِنْ أَعْظَمِ الْقَسَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Bijāhi man baytuhu fī ṭaybatin ḥaramun
Wasmuhu qasamun min aʿẓamil qasami
verses.english:
By the rank of him whose house is a sanctum in Ṭayba [Medina]
And whose name is an oath—from the greatest of oaths!
verses.singable:
By the prestige of him who the Goodly Land is his home;
Whose very name is an oath of greatest immensity.

verses.arabic:
وَهَذِهِ بُرْدَةُ المُخْتَارِ قَدْ خُتِمَتْ
وَالحَمْدُ للهِ فِي بَدْءٍ وَفِي خَتَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Wa hādhihi burdatul mukhtāri qad khutimat
Walḥamdu liLlāhi fī badʾin wa fī khatami
verses.english:
This Burda Ode of the Chosen One is thus complete
And all praise is due to Allah in its beginning and its end.
verses.singable:
This is the Burdah of the Selected One, now complete;
And praise Allah at the start and finish, eternally!

verses.arabic:
أَبْيَاتُهَا قَدْ أَتَتْ سِتِّينَ مَعْ مِائَةٍ
فَرِّجْ بِهَا كَرْبَنَا يَا وَاسِعَ الْكَرَمِ
verses.transliteration:
Abyātuhā qad atat sittīna maʿ miʾatin
Farrij bihā karbanā yā wāsiʿal karami
verses.english:
Its couplets—numbering one hundred and sixty—have come;
Relieve us our difficulties by them, O Vastly Generous!
verses.singable:
The number of verses in it is one sixty or more;
Relieve by them our woes, O Vast in Gen'rosity!
